title: Paronychia tenella
intro: Paronychia tenella is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Nyctaginaceae family, commonly known as four o'clock flower. The health benefits of Paronychia tenella include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Paronychia tenella include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Paronychia tenella are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Paronychia tenella is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Paronychia and is a type of annual or biennial herb. The species is native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, and parts of Central America. Paronychia tenella is a small plant that typically grows to be around 10-30 cm in height, with white or pink flowers. Its classification places it within the larger group of plants known as eudicots.
